title: Skeptic Assignment Manifest
description: The aggregate assignment manifest skeptic_setup.py writes (default {durable_root}/skeptic/runs/{RUN_ID}/assignments.json) BEFORE any codex dispatch, so 'every assigned entity was checked' is provable (coverage discipline). It is the ground truth --verify-merged asserts the merged skeptic_triage.json accounts for. producer_input_digest is carried verbatim from the worklist (the freshness chain), input_digest is this run's own skeptic digest (see skeptic_setup.py). Strict at every level.

run_id.description: Skeptic RUN_ID (own resume domain -- NOT a mass/glossary run id).

input_digest.description: This run's skeptic input_digest (see skeptic_setup.py -- includes LanguageConfig.raw_bytes, skeptic_constants.py, and the full skeptic code closure).

producer_input_digest.description: Copied verbatim from the suspicion_worklist.json this run consumed (freshness chain).

canon_sha256.description: Optional. `compute_frozen_input_hash_from_state(state, content)` -- sha256 hex over the frozen canon.json's own STATE TAG ('absent'/'regular'/'irregular', never just 'present', as ASCII bytes) then its content bytes, joined by exactly ONE NUL byte in between (2 parts, NUL-separated -- NOT NUL-terminated: no trailing NUL after the content bytes, unlike producer_input_digest's own multi-part formula in suspicion-worklist.schema.json). NOT a hash of raw content alone: that scheme would make an absent file, a genuinely-empty regular file, and a directory/dangling-symlink all hash identically, silently defeating the tripwire below for exactly those substitutions. The `(state, content)` pair is the SNAPSHOT skeptic_setup.py captured ONCE at derivation-read time -- before the freshness/worklist check that same snapshot fed -- and is never a later re-read at the moment this aggregate is written; this value describes what canon.json WAS when this run's assignments were derived, not necessarily what a fresh read of it would return an instant later. Two independent verification modes re-hash the on-disk canon.json fresh and fail on mismatch: `skeptic_ready.py --verify-merged` (after a successful merge) and `skeptic_ready.py --check-frozen-inputs` (a standalone mode, reached instead when every batch's own fragment failed to become ready and merge/verify is never attempted) -- a BEST-EFFORT integrity tripwire that catches ACCIDENTAL / non-adversarial mutation of the frozen input (a crash mid-write, a stray process, a well-behaved-but-buggy agent). It is NOT sound against a prompt-injected skeptic agent: that agent has pipeline-wide filesystem write (FS containment is out of scope here) and can rewrite or delete this co-located stamp to match its tampered canon. A sound version (anchoring the setup-time hash in a trusted channel) is deferred to Phase 3.

manifest_sha256.description: Optional. Same `compute_frozen_input_hash_from_state` state-tagged formula and derivation-time-snapshot semantics as canon_sha256 (see its own description for the full algorithm and both verification modes), computed for the manifest.json this run was set up against.

senses_sha256.description: Optional (#243). Same `compute_frozen_input_hash_from_state` state-tagged formula and derivation-time-snapshot semantics as canon_sha256 (see its own description) -- an absent sidecar hashes DISTINCTLY from a genuinely-empty regular one (never collapsing to the same value the way a plain content-only hash would), computed for the canon_senses.json sidecar this run was set up against. The sidecar became a THIRD frozen input once `--verify-merged` started PARSING it to project the ambiguity-competitors universe, so it needs the same tamper tripwire as canon_sha256/manifest_sha256 -- but `--check-frozen-inputs` never parses any of the three (canon.json/manifest.json/canon_senses.json alike): it re-hashes raw bytes plus path state ONLY, deliberately, so it can still answer even when a frozen input is malformed or absent, which is the whole reason that mode exists.

assignments.description: One per entity to be skeptically examined; the assigned SET is the coverage universe --verify-merged checks the merged triage against.

assignment_id.description: Stable id = sha256 hex of the NFC source_form -- RNG-free, resume-stable, the join key every triage record carries back.

windows.description: The bounded CITABLE windows fed to the skeptic (origin='block' occurrences only; capped at windows_per_entity). Each window's `block` is fed WHOLE (manifest.blocks[block].plain_text); char_start/char_end mark the occurrence within it. A propose_split/adverse verdict's evidence offsets must fall inside one of these windows.

windows_truncated.description: true when the entity had more citable windows than windows_per_entity, so some were dropped -- the skeptic MAY then return insufficient_window/escalate honestly rather than claiming full coverage.
